operatingMedium: Fresh or brackish water, 10–30 °C, 0.5–3 bar, DN15–DN50 piping [SRC: VN_finfish_farming]

assessments.rationale: RAS circulates water (fresh or brackish) — the sensor's exact design medium. Particulate suspensions (fish waste, biofilm fragments) are acoustically transparent and within tolerance.
RAS water temperatures (10-30 degC for all species) are 58+ degC below the 90 degC limit. Gap >2.8x — no risk under any operating scenario.
RAS piping pressures (0.5-3 bar) are 3-20x below the 10 bar limit. Water hammer resistance >16 bar provides additional margin.
Lowest RAS flow (small fingerling tank, ~5 l/min) exceeds the DN20 minimum (4.2 l/min). Main loop flows are 100-12,000 l/min. No measurement gap.

RAS includes active aeration and oxygenation stages that introduce gas into water. Main loop downstream of degassers is bubble-free, but sensor must be positioned correctly. Standard installation guidance resolves this.
assessments.mitigation: Install sensor downstream of CO2 stripper / degasser where water is bubble-free; Ensure pump suction design prevents vortex air entrainment; Use sensor built-in bubble detection diagnostic as early warning for abnormal air entrainment

assessments.rationale: All closed-pipe sections in the RAS loop are pressurized and fully filled during normal operation. Empty-pipe detection provides additional safety for pump failure events.
RAS piping layouts vary; compact or containerized systems may have elbows close to measurement points. 100-250 mm straight run is achievable in most layouts. Flow conditioner solves edge cases.
assessments.mitigation: Include 100-250 mm straight-run requirement in RAS piping design guidelines; For compact or retrofit installations, add vane-type flow conditioner upstream ($20-50 part)

assessments.rationale: RAS temperature is tightly controlled (+-1 degC). Built-in NTC compensation handles this with 0.2% residual error. No extreme thermal transients in aquaculture.

result.marketStatus: SURVIVING — no knockouts; passes all constraints
